body:
## Summary
- Change the orchestrator planner `permission_mode` from `approve-reads` to `approve-all`
- Document the orchestrator route permission mode in request lifecycle docs
- Add a workflow contract assertion for the planner permission mode

## Verification
- `npm run build`
- `node --test dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`

Closes #48

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced PR #49 for issue #48; an automated review is warranted before stopping.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777521563961 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDY1MzU0MzI6aW1wbGVtZW50OjQ4OnJldmlldzo0OToz -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | PR changes only `.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml`, two relevant docs files, and one existing workflow contract test assertion. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation is the minimal requested change: `permission_mode: approve-reads` to `permission_mode: approve-all`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/architecture/request-lifecycle.md` to add the orchestrator route and `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` to describe planner `approve-all` behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR body closes #48; issue #48 discussion explicitly requested the quick fix and linked the request-lifecycle permission table. The PR updates that exact documented table. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | No new implementation path or duplicate helper was added; the PR uses the existing `run-agent-task` workflow input and existing `envelope.test.ts` contract test style. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | The permission broadening is scoped to planner tool access. The orchestrator docs still state planner output is validated against fixed transition policy and max-round budget before dispatch; the PR does not weaken delegated route authorization behavior. |

## Notes

- The PR satisfies the requested quick fix while documenting the broader planner tool access in both the lifecycle table and orchestrator technical details.
- The added assertion in `envelope.test.ts` is focused and directly guards the workflow contract that regressed.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** The chosen quick fix intentionally broadens planner tool access to `approve-all`, matching the user’s explicit request and the documented context-gathering route pattern. The longer-term “prefetch context before planning” option from issue #48 remains outside this PR’s scope.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #49 implements the quick fix requested from issue #48 after the orchestrator planner failed in a non-interactive workflow while trying to run GitHub read commands under `approve-reads`.
- The PR changes the orchestrator planner `permission_mode` to `approve-all` so it can gather current PR, issue, and repository context during GitHub Actions runs.
- It also documents the orchestrator route permission mode and adds a workflow contract assertion to guard the behavior.
- Reviewers agree this matches the explicit user request, while noting that a longer-term read-only context bundle or command allowlist would be cleaner future work.

## Review
No reviewer raised a BLOCKING or WARNING issue. The only concern is informational: this is an intentional permission broadening chosen as the requested quick fix, with the more constrained design left out of scope.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues | INFO | Both reviewer inputs found no required branch changes. The broader planner permission is intentional and documented. |

## Progress
- The PR already updates `.github/workflows/agent-orchestrator.yml` from `approve-reads` to `approve-all`.
- Documentation was updated in the request lifecycle and orchestrator technical details docs.
- A focused workflow contract assertion was added in `envelope.test.ts`.
- Existing PR discussion already acknowledges the tradeoff between the quick fix and the longer-term constrained planner design.
- No inline comments were posted because there are no concrete, non-duplicative, line-specific issues.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: no unresolved actionable work remains from the available reviews.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work.

title: Use approve-all for orchestrator planner
